_comment: Maps tables to their preferred lookup columns for rdb-selects.sql WHERE clause generation. Use when multiple candidate keys exist and you want to specify which column should be used in the WHERE predicate.
_usage: Reference this file when reviewing generated rdb-selects.sql or update it to guide generate_query_expected.py toward correct predicates for multi-key tables.

dbo.D_PATIENT.reason: D_PATIENT is the patient dimension and is keyed by person LOCAL_ID

dbo.EVENT_METRIC.reason: EVENT_METRIC is keyed by the observation LOCAL_ID, not by individual observation instance variables

dbo.EVENT_METRIC_INC.reason: EVENT_METRIC_INC (incremental) is keyed by observation LOCAL_ID

dbo.MORBIDITY_REPORT.reason: MORBIDITY_REPORT is keyed by the morbidity report LOCAL_ID (observation derived)

dbo.MORBIDITY_REPORT_EVENT.reason: MORBIDITY_REPORT_EVENT tracks events by patient; PATIENT_KEY is the business key. Other keys like NURSING_HOME_KEY, PHYSICIAN_KEY, etc. are dimensional references, not identity.
